behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure is most likely at module handoff (request builder -> API client), not pure algorithm logic. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit was considered, but behavior text says "request sent to the external API" (wire-level payload), so a pure function test can miss dropped fields during module wiring. System rejected because no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this; a stub client capturing outgoing payload is sufficient. Agentic/Workflow rejected because correctness does not depend on model judgment or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake in-process API client that records outbound payload. Call classify with a taxonomy and assert the outbound request contains the full label list exactly as configured.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, because this is output normalization/guard logic on one response value. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration considered, but specific behavior text focuses on mapping invalid label -> "unknown"; this can be fully exercised in a local normalization/decision function with no module boundary dependency required. System/Agentic/Workflow rejected: no real infra, no model-quality dependence, no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/label-validation function with taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other] and raw label "sports"; assert result is "unknown" and no passthrough occurs.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, since determinism of normalization is a local pure-logic property. PASS2-AUDIT: Higher levels rejected because behavior text explicitly scopes to "identical raw API responses" and "normalization step"; no external I/O, no inter-module handoff requirement, no model decision quality check.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization multiple times with the exact same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants if relevant) and assert stable identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because behavior explicitly requires "repeated live API calls" and label consistency depends on real model/provider outputs. PASS2-AUDIT: System was considered (real HTTP), but disqualified as final because the core assertion is model-output consistency, not only transport/infrastructure. Workflow rejected: repeated calls are not a multi-step agent pipeline with sequential model decisions; each call is a single classification decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider calls with identical input. Record provider raw responses and final labels. Assert final labels are stable whenever raw provider response is unchanged; allow divergence only when provider response content differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure typically appears across config + request builder + dispatch gate interaction. PASS2-AUDIT with Integration-brake: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? No. (b) real AI output needed? No, this is pre-dispatch enforcement. (c) multi-agent chain? No. So Integration stands. Unit considered, but behavior text says "before dispatch" with policy effect (truncate/reject), which is safer to validate at module wiring boundary where accidental dispatch can occur.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget and use a long document. With a fake client, assert policy outcome: either truncated payload sent within budget or request rejected and client not called, per configured policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because this requires real provider-reported usage from a real AI API call. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected since mocks can hide provider-side tokenization/accounting drift. System considered (real HTTP), but behavior risk is tied to real model/provider accounting behavior exposed by AI API responses, so Agentic is the stronger minimum under LLM-DEP guidance. Workflow rejected because this is a single call check, not multi-step agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls with representative documents and capture provider usage metadata. Assert reported total/input/output tokens are <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure is retry orchestration across classifier + client + backoff policy modules. PASS2-AUDIT with Integration-brake: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? Not strictly; stubbed rate-limit response and controllable clock can reproduce. (b) real AI output needed? No, only status/error handling. (c) multi-agent chain? No. System/Agentic/Workflow therefore rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake API client that returns rate-limit error on first call and success on second, with a fake clock/sleeper. Assert module performs wait/backoff, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
